question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here is the full list of hook events a charm can observe, grouped by category:

---

## Core Lifecycle Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `install` | Runs once when unit provisioning is complete |
| `start` | Runs after initialization is complete |
| `config-changed` | Runs when configuration or IP address changes |
| `upgrade-charm` | Runs when the charm code is updated |
| `update-status` | Periodic status check (every 5 minutes by default) |
| `stop` | Runs when unit removal is initiated |
| `remove` | Final hook before unit destruction |

---

## Relation Hooks
*(where `<endpoint>` is the name of the relation endpoint)*
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<endpoint>-relation-created` | Two applications have become related |
| `<endpoint>-relation-joined` | A related unit has joined the relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-changed` | Relation data has been updated |
| `<endpoint>-relation-departed` | A related unit has left the relation |
| `<endpoint>-relation-broken` | The relation is ending entirely |

---

## Leadership Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `leader-elected` | This unit has been elected leader |
| `leader-settings-changed` | Non-leader units are notified of leadership changes |

---

## Storage Hooks
*(where `<storage>` is the name of the storage directive)*
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<storage>-storage-attached` | A storage volume is ready to use |
| `<storage>-storage-detaching` | A storage volume is being removed |

---

## Secret Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `secret-changed` | A new secret revision is available (consumers) |
| `secret-rotate` | The secret needs to be updated with a new revision |
| `secret-remove` | An outdated secret revision can be safely deleted |
| `secret-expired` | A secret revision has reached its expiration time |

---

## Workload / Pebble Hooks *(Kubernetes only)*
*(where `<container>` is the name of the container)*
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<container>-pebble-ready` | The container's Pebble API is ready |
| `<container>-pebble-custom-notice` | A custom Pebble notice has occurred |
| `<container>-pebble-check-failed` | A Pebble health check has failed |
| `<container>-pebble-check-recovered` | A previously failed health check has recovered |

---

## Upgrade Series Hooks *(machine charms)*
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `pre-series-upgrade` | Fired before an OS series upgrade begins |
| `post-series-upgrade` | Fired after an OS series upgrade completes |

---

That's **30+ distinct hook events** (with relation, storage, and Pebble hooks multiplied by the number of endpoints/storages/containers defined in `metadata.yaml`).
